ab: ray ’ima ’ol’olae’an niya’om taew’an ray walo’, kiSka:at ila, ba:aw ila ray to’ing, ’isa:a ray raehoran ila nanaw rowa:Sek.
ch: 小時候我們家住在東河，上學以後，就搬到了桃園，一直在都市裡生活。
en: When we were young, our family lived in Donghe. After I started school, we moved to Taoyuan and have been living in the city ever since.

ab: kiSka:at ila ray kam’ibabaw kakiSka:atan, yako pawhi:il ila ’aehae’ kakiSka:aten noka SinSinraeho’ kaspengan. Sekla’ ila ray taywan SinSinraeho’ hayza’ ’akoy ka ’ima ’okik hingha’ ka kaspengan.
ch: 上了大學以後，我修了一堂關於原住民文化的課程，瞭解到臺灣原住民族有各式各樣豐富的文化。
en: After I went to college, I took a course on aboriginal culture and learned that the aboriginal people in Taiwan have various and rich cultures.

ab: ’isa:a, ’isahini tinal’omaeh ray ’ima ’inaro’ ka kaSangayan, yako ki minatini’ kamamanra:an lobih ray ’asang somingozaw ka tatini’, ’am Sekla’ ka ’in nonak a kaspengan.
ch: 因此，今年暑假時，我和哥哥回到部落訪問老人家，想要瞭解自己族群的文化。
en: So this summer, my brother and I went back to our tribe to visit the elderly, hoping to understand the culture of our own ethnic group.

ab: tatini’ monmaykaSpat ila tinal’omaeh, ma’ kita’en nak ‘al’alak naehan.
ch: 老人家已經八十歲了，但是看起來還像中年人。
en: The old man is already eighty years old, but he still looks like a middle-aged man.

ab: ya:o somingzaw hi siya ra:am piza’ kapayaka:i’, siya malnoSySiyat ra:am a tomal, ma’ ra:am ’oyaeh malnoSaypapa:aS, malnomoto: ki malnoripon.
ch: 我問他會哪些語言，他的賽夏語說得很流利，也會說泰雅語、客家話和日本話。
en: I asked him which languages he knew, and he was fluent in Saisiyat, as well as Atayal, Hakka and Japanese.

ab: yako somingozaw hi siya ’isa:a ra:am ila, siya ’ima ’ol’olae’an kiSka:at ka ’alnoripon, minkoringan nisiya Saypapa:aS, siya ray kapatawawan Sekla’ ’akoy ka moto:, ’isa:a siya ra:am ’akoy ka kapayaka:i’.
ch: 我問他才知道，他小時候就學日本話，太太是泰雅族人，而他工作上認識許多客家人，所以他會說這麼多語言。
en: I asked him to find out that he learned Japanese when he was a child, his wife is an Atayal, and he knows many Hakkas at work, so he can speak so many languages.

ab: yami ’ima somingozaw, tatini’ manabih ’akoy nisiya kinra:aman nom ’omalep ka howaw.
ch: 我們訪談的時候，老人家談論了許多關於狩獵的傳統知識。
en: During our interview, the old man talked about a lot of traditional knowledge about hunting.

ab: ’ana yami haSa’ baezae’, niya’om noka laleke: ’alingo’on, ’isa:a kayzaeh hayza’ naehan kabaezae’en.
ch: 雖然我們沒有辦法完全聽懂，但是我們有用手機錄影，可以再仔細聆聽。
en: Although we could’t fully understand it, we could record it on our cell phones and listen to it more.

ab: ’ima ’ol’olae’an ’okik Sekla’ ka ’in nonak a kaspengan, komita’ nonak ma’ SaySiyat kaSikar a tomal.
ch: 小時候我不瞭解自己族群的文化，對於自己是原住民有點不好意思。
en: When I was young, I didn’t understand the culture of my ethnic group, and I was a little embarrassed that I was an aborigine.

ab: Sekla’ kin’akoy, nonak Sapang SaySiyat, sa’owaz maynonak a tomal, ma’ kayzaeh patopa: ma’an Sapang ka Sinrahoe’.
ch: 瞭解愈多以後，愈覺得自己身為賽夏族人，是很獨特的，也很值得驕傲。
en: The more I know, the more I feel that being a Saisiyat is unique and something to be proud of.
